mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during post-shock recovery periods, with IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader economic deceleration, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >50% of triggered instances despite the housing contraction signal, invalidating the predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue expectations and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict return direction with an accuracy above 52% over rolling 3-year windows, or produces negative returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and increases discount rates, generating negative forward S&P 500 returns as investors demand higher risk premiums.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation observations, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, driving positive equity returns as earnings expectations rise and valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: The joint demand signal produces negative returns in >60% of cases during late-cycle expansions, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.
